sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: 엘리번스 헬스, 2026년 1분기 영업수익 495억 달러로 1.5% 성장·기대 초과, 조정 희석 EPS 12.58달러·연간 가이던스 상향

keyPoints.text: 1분기 영업수익이 495억 달러로 전년 동기 대비 1.5% 증가하며 기대치를 상회했습니다.
조정 희석 주당순이익은 12.58달러로 강한 운영 실적과 주당 약 1달러의 비반복 투자이익이 기여했습니다.
2026년 연간 GAAP 희석 주당순이익 가이던스를 최소 19.85달러로 상향했습니다.

riskChanges.text: 급여비용률이 86.8%로 전년 대비 40포인트 상승했으며, 메디케이드 의료비 증가 추세가 주요 요인이었습니다.

businessChanges.text: CEO 게일 부드로는 1분기 실적이 기대를 초과했으며 청구 경험 개선이 확인됐다고 밝혔습니다.
운영비용률 12.8%에는 9억 3,500만 달러 규모의 비용 발생이 포함됐습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 1분기 영업수익은 495억 달러로 전년 동기 대비 1.5% 증가했습니다.
조정 희석 주당순이익 12.58달러는 강한 운영 결과와 비반복 투자이익 1달러의 합산 효과입니다.
급여비용률 86.8%는 메디케어 성과 개선이 메디케이드 비용 증가를 일부 상쇄한 결과입니다.
청구지급일(Days in Claims Payable)은 46.6일로 2025년 말 대비 5.3일 증가했습니다.

watchItems.text: 메디케이드 의료비 증가 추세가 지속되는 가운데 급여비용률 관리와 메디케어 성과 개선 지속 여부가 핵심입니다.
연간 가이던스 19.85달러 달성 여부는 하반기 청구 경험 안정화에 달려 있습니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: EX-99.1 2 a1q2026elvearningsrelease.htm EX-99.1 Document E A R N I N G S R E L E A S E ELEVANCE HEALTH REPORTS FIRST QUARTER 2026 RESULTS; RAISES FULL-YEAR GUIDANCE • 1Q 2026 operating revenue of $49.5 billion, up 1.5% from 1Q 2025 • 1Q 2026 diluted EPS 1 of $8.00; adjusted diluted EPS 2 of $12.58 driven by strong operating results and ~$1 per share of non-recurring investment income • FY 2026 diluted EPS 1 guidance to be at least $19.85, including the Company's estimate of the financial impact for the CMS matter • FY 2026 adjusted diluted EPS 2 guidance raised to at least $26.75, supported by underlying business strength, actions to reduce medical costs, and increased visibility • Reaffirm FY 2026 operating cash flow of at least $5.5 billion, inclusive of potential  Normalized numeric aliases: $49.5 billion = 495억 달러; $5.5 billion = 55억 달러; $1.5 billion = 15억 달러; $49.5 = 49.5달러; $8.00 = 8.00달러; $12.58 = 12.58달러; $1 = 1달러; $19.85 = 19.85달러; $5.5 = 5.5달러; $26.75 = 26.75달러; $1.5 = 1.5달러.

2026 - Elevance Health, Inc. (NYSE: ELV) reported first quarter 2026 results ahead of expectations. "Our first quarter results exceeded expectations, reflecting underlying business strength and improving claims experience. We are raising our full-year adjusted EPS guidance, supported by greater visibility into the balance of the year. Our actions are driving more consistent performance and position Elevance Health for continued improvement over time.” Gail K. Boudreaux President and Chief Executive Officer 1. Earnings per diluted share ("EPS"). 2. Refer to GAAP reconciliation tables on pages 13 and 14 herein for reconciliation of GAAP to adjusted measures. 1 Elevance Health Consolidated Enterprise Highlights (Unaudited) (In billions) Three Months Ended March 31, 2026 March 31, 2025 Operating Revenue 1 $49.5 $48.8 Operating Gain notes 1 and 2 $2.1 $3.2 Adjusted Operating Gain notes 1 and  Normalized numeric aliases: $49.5 = 49.5달러; $48.8 = 48.8달러; $2.1 = 2.1달러; $3.2 = 3.2달러.

3 $3.2 $3.3 Operating Margin 1 4.2 % 6.5 % Adjusted Operating Margin notes 1 and 3 6.5 % 6.7 % 1. See “Basis of Presentation” on page 5 herein. 2. Operating Gain for the three months ended March 31, 2026, and March 31, 2025, include items that are excluded from adjusted shareholders' net income. See "GAAP Reconciliation" on pages 13 and 14 herein. 3. Adjusted Operating Gain for the three months ended March 31, 2026, and March 31, 2025, exclude items that are excluded from adjusted shareholders' net income. See "GAAP Reconciliation" on pages 13 and 14 herein. Operating revenue was $49.5 billion in the first quarter of 2026, an increase of $0.7 billion, or 1.5 percent compared to the prior year quarter. This was driven by higher premium yields in our Health Benefits segment and growth in CarelonRx product revenue, partially offset by  Normalized numeric aliases: $49.5 billion = 495억 달러; $0.7 billion = 7억 달러; $3.2 = 3.2달러; $3.3 = 3.3달러; $49.5 = 49.5달러; $0.7 = 0.7달러; 1.5 percent = 1.5%.

and Employer Group risk membership. The benefit expense ratio was 86.8 percent, an increase of 40 basis points, reflecting expected elevated medical cost trend in our Medicaid business, partially offset by improved performance in Medicare. Days in Claims Payable stood at 46.6 days as of March 31, 2026, an increase of 5.3 days from December 31, 2025 and an increase of 3.8 days year over year. The operating expense ratio of 12.8 percent included a $935 million accrual representing our current best estimate of the identified potential exposure related to the CMS notice. The Company also recorded a charge of $129 million related to business optimization as we simplify and enhance our organizational structure. The adjusted operating expense ratio was 10.5 percent,  Normalized numeric aliases: $935 million = 9억 3,500만 달러; $129 million = 1억 2,900만 달러; $935 = 935달러; $129 = 129달러; 86.8 percent = 86.8%; 12.8 percent = 12.8%; 10.5 percent = 10.5%; 40 basis points = 40포인트; 20 basis points = 20포인트.
